title: FAQ: NUwave-guest access
plain_text: NUwave-guest is Northeastern's open-access, unsecured, wireless network designed to provide temporary internet access for campus guests. Table of Contents What is required to register for temporary access to NUwave-guest? How long is the access period for NUwave-guest? How many devices can I register? Can I register access to NUwave-guest for conference or event guests ahead of time? How does a visiting scholar, researcher, lecturer, contractor, or longer-term guest access the network? What is required to register for temporary access to NUwave-guest? You can register for a temporary guest wireless account through the Guest Wireless Portal registration page. Provide: Your name Phone number Email address You will receive a text message containing the username and a password to be entered into the guest login page for network access on up to three devices. Refer to How do I register for individual NUwave-guest access? for detailed registration instructions. How long is the access period for NUwave-guest? There is a 30-day limit for NUwave-guest connections. Once the allotted time expires, you will be required to re-register for a new password to continue use on the guest wireless network. How many devices can I register? A registered account can be used with up to 3 personal devices. Can I register access to NUwave-guest for conference or event guests ahead of time? Yes. NUwave-guest provides wireless internet access to short-term guests such as parents, vendors, and event or conference guests. Read How do I provide WiFi to conference and event guests? to learn more about granting access to event or single/multi-day conference guests. How does a visiting scholar, researcher, lecturer, contractor, or longer-term guest access the network? If someone is a visiting scholar, researcher, lecturer, contractor, or longer-term guest, they will need to work with their Northeastern contact to obtain a sponsored account for on-campus internet access. Refer to the following articles for more information on obtaining a sponsored account: What is a sponsored account? How do I submit a sponsored account request?
body_html:
NUwave-guest is Northeastern's open-access, unsecured, wireless network designed to provide temporary internet access for campus guests.

Table of Contents

  • What is required to register for temporary access to NUwave-guest?
  • How long is the access period for NUwave-guest?
  • How many devices can I register?
  • Can I register access to NUwave-guest for conference or event guests ahead of time?
  • How does a visiting scholar, researcher, lecturer, contractor, or longer-term guest access the network?

What is required to register for temporary access to NUwave-guest?

You can register for a temporary guest wireless account through the Guest Wireless Portal registration page. Provide:

  • Your name
  • Phone number
  • Email address

You will receive a text message containing the username and a password to be entered into the guest login page for network access on up to three devices. Refer to How do I register for individual NUwave-guest access? for detailed registration instructions.

How long is the access period for NUwave-guest?

There is a 30-day limit for NUwave-guest connections. Once the allotted time expires, you will be required to re-register for a new password to continue use on the guest wireless network.

How many devices can I register?

A registered account can be used with up to 3 personal devices.

Can I register access to NUwave-guest for conference or event guests ahead of time?

Yes. NUwave-guest provides wireless internet access to short-term guests such as parents, vendors, and event or conference guests. Read How do I provide WiFi to conference and event guests? to learn more about granting access to event or single/multi-day conference guests.

How does a visiting scholar, researcher, lecturer, contractor, or longer-term guest access the network?

If someone is a visiting scholar, researcher, lecturer, contractor, or longer-term guest, they will need to work with their Northeastern contact to obtain a sponsored account for on-campus internet access. Refer to the following articles for more information on obtaining a sponsored account:

  • What is a sponsored account?
  • How do I submit a sponsored account request?
